summary: The Goshen Township Zoning Commission held a public hearing on two zoning cases. The commission re-approved an expired preliminary development plan for the Pebble Grove PUD subdivision (306 units on 112 acres) and then approved the final development plan with staff conditions. The commission also approved a text amendment establishing regulations for temporary dwellings during new construction or major renovations, originally prompted by tornado-related needs.
decisions: Approved video meeting minutes from February 6, 2024
Approved extension/re-approval of the expired preliminary development plan for Pebble Grove subdivision (ZC 2024-004)
Approved final development plan for Pebble Grove subdivision (ZC 2024-004) with five staff-recommended conditions
Approved text amendment ZC 2024-003 regarding temporary dwellings during construction or major renovations
Motion to adjourn at 7:25 PM approved
action_items: Developer (Grand Communities) to submit HOA legal documentation for maintaining open space
Developer to update final development plans with detailed list of proposed amenities in open space areas
Developer to meet all national and local requirements for modification of wetlands and soils
Developer to provide additional details for proposed home models including site plans, elevations, and materials
Developer to submit a lighting plan meeting zoning resolution requirements
Staff to look into the status of the Appalachian grant for the Goshen Road to Linton Road connector road
Developer to provide private covenants before construction begins
key_amounts: 112 acres total site for Pebble Grove subdivision
306 total residential units (220 detached single-family, 86 attached/duplex)
1,400 square feet minimum floor area for dwelling units
$300,000 range for Maple Street single-family homes
50 foot minimum lot width, 30 foot front yard setback
Up to 1 year temporary dwelling permit for new construction, 6 months for major renovations
notable_topics: Re-approval of expired preliminary development plan for Pebble Grove PUD subdivision
Final development plan review for Pebble Grove subdivision with 306 units
Stormwater management and retention/detention pond design
Traffic study results showing no improvements required for Linton Road
Potential connector road from Linton Road to Goshen Road through school/park property
Public sewer and water service from Clermont County
Text amendment for temporary dwellings during construction or after disaster
Resident concerns about school capacity, property taxes, and failed school levies
Pond safety and fencing concerns from residents
attendees: Mr. Frisbee (Zoning Commission member - absent/no response at roll call)
Mr. Ottke (Zoning Commission member - absent/no response at roll call)
Zach Clark (Cardinal Engineering, civil engineering consultant for applicant)
notes: Two zoning commission members (Frisbee and Ottke) appeared absent as they did not respond during roll call. The preliminary development plan had expired in August 2023 with no notification sent to the applicant. The developer changed engineers midway through the project causing delays. A grant application for a connector road through school/park property to Goshen Road was submitted jointly by the school district and township but status is unknown. Choice One Engineering designed the connector road plans. The Clermont County Planning Commission had already recommended approval of the text amendment. Next meeting scheduled for May 7, 2024 with a possible text amendment on the agenda.
title: ZC 4-2-24
